nodes.context: Chechnya-based paramilitary forces loyal to Ramzan Kadyrov, involved in human rights abuses and conflicts in Syria and Ukraine.
Former separatist fighters from the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria, many of whom were pardoned and joined the Kadyrovites.
Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014.
Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014.
Ukrainian National Guard unit based in Mariupol, known for fighting against Chechen troops.
Pro-Russian separatist forces in eastern Ukraine, reported to have been executed by Chechen troops for refusing to fight.
A Kadyrovite unit headed by Adam Delimkhanov, involved in kidnappings.
Former FSB spetsnaz unit headed by Movladi Baisarov, disbanded after conflict with Ramzan Kadyrov.
Spetsnaz GRU unit headed by Said-Magomed Kakiev, sided with Baisarov against Kadyrovites.

edges.context: Many former Ichkeria militants were pardoned and integrated into the Kadyrovites.
The Vostok Battalion is a Chechen unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of the Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
The Chechen Death Battalion is a Chechen unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of the Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
Chechen troops fought against the Azov Regiment during the Siege of Mariupol.
Chechen troops reportedly executed conscripted soldiers of the Luhansk People's Republic who refused to fight.
Oil Regiment is a unit within the Kadyrovites, headed by Adam Delimkhanov.
After Akhmad Kadyrov's death, conflict with Ramzan Kadyrov led to Goretz being declared outlaw and its leader killed.
Special Battalion Zapad sided with Goretz during a confrontation with Kadyrovites.
